title: A Conserved C-Terminal Region in Gp71 of the Small Isometric-Head Phage LL-H and ORF474 of the Prolate-Head Phage JCL1032 Is Implicated in Specificity of Adsorption of Phage to Its Host, Lactobacillus delbrueckii
summary: The paper describes the morphology of three phages: LL-H and LL-S are described as 'small isometric-head' phages, while JCL1032 is described as a 'prolate-head' phage. The study focuses on adsorption mechanisms and host-range mutants of phage LL-H, with several mutants (LL-H-a7, a8, a21, a23, a24, and LL-H(Bes-1)) derived from it, but no additional morphological details are provided for these variants.

LL-H.structural_proteins: major tail protein (encoded by gene g17), minor tail protein (encoded by gene g71)

LL-H(Bes-1).host_bacterium: Lactobacillus delbrueckii subsp. lactis ATCC 15808 (specifically strain Bes-1)

LL-H-a7.host_bacterium: Lactobacillus delbrueckii subsp. lactis ATCC 15808 (and strain Ads-5)

LL-H-a8.host_bacterium: Lactobacillus delbrueckii subsp. lactis ATCC 15808 (and strain Ads-5)

LL-H-a21.host_bacterium: Lactobacillus delbrueckii subsp. lactis ATCC 15808 (and strain Ads-5)

LL-H-a23.host_bacterium: Lactobacillus delbrueckii subsp. lactis ATCC 15808 (and strain Ads-5)

LL-H-a24.host_bacterium: Lactobacillus delbrueckii subsp. lactis ATCC 15808 (and strain Ads-5)
